nodes.title: Reinforcement Learning Overview
nodes.description: The center of decision making and goal-oriented behaviors.

nodes.title: Agent
nodes.description: The learner and decision maker. Explores states, chooses actions, and tries to maximize expected long-term rewards.

nodes.title: Environment
nodes.description: The external system the agent interacts with. Responds to actions by transitioning states and providing objective reward signals.

nodes.title: Reward Signal
nodes.description: The scalar feedback indicating immediate success. Used mathematically by the agent to define their primary objective.

nodes.title: Policy Core (π)
nodes.description: The behavior formula of the agent at a given time. It formally defines what action should be taken in each given state.

nodes.title: Value Function
nodes.description: An estimation of what a state or action is fundamentally worth in terms of long-term discounted future rewards.

nodes.title: Model (Optional)
nodes.description: The agent's internal representation of the environment. Used to predict what the environment will do next for planning.

nodes.title: Action Space (A)
nodes.description: The complete set of choices available. Can be discrete (buttons on a controller) or continuous (steering a wheel).

nodes.title: State Space (S)
nodes.description: All possible configurations of the environment. Can be massive or infinite depending on complexity.

nodes.title: State Representation
nodes.description: How the agent practically perceives the environment's state, often via sensors, camera pixels, or structured memory.

nodes.title: Environment Dynamics
nodes.description: The underlying mathematical rules determining how the state changes in response to actions.

nodes.title: Policy Gradient Methods
nodes.description: Optimizing a parameterized policy directly via gradient ascent on the expected cumulative reward.

nodes.title: State Value (V)
nodes.description: The expected return when starting in state S and following the current policy π thereafter.

nodes.title: Action Value (Q)
nodes.description: The expected return when starting in state S, taking action A, and then following the policy π.

nodes.title: Transition Model
nodes.description: A predictive model estimating the probability distribution of the next state given a state and action.

nodes.title: Reward Model
nodes.description: A predictive model estimating the expected immediate reward given a state and an action.

nodes.title: Planning
nodes.description: Using models to simulate future scenarios and compute optimal policies without interacting with the real world.

nodes.title: Exploration
nodes.description: Trying new, potentially unoptimal actions to gather more information and find better long-term paths.

nodes.title: Exploitation
nodes.description: Executing known actions with the highest expected reward value according to current knowledge.

nodes.title: Deterministic Policy
nodes.description: A function that maps each state to exactly one specific action uniquely.

nodes.title: Stochastic Policy
nodes.description: A probability distribution output mapping states to a likelihood over a range of possible actions.

nodes.title: Bellman Equation
nodes.description: The recursive relationship for value functions, decomposing a value into immediate reward plus discounted future value.

nodes.title: Temporal Difference (TD)
nodes.description: Learning from incomplete episodes by bootstrapping updates off of other previously estimated values.

nodes.title: Q-Learning
nodes.description: An off-policy TD control algorithm. It directly approximates the optimal action-value function regardless of the policy being followed.

nodes.title: Deep Q-Network (DQN)
nodes.description: Using deep neural networks to approximate complex Q-values in massive state spaces. Famously solved Atari games.

nodes.title: Proximal Policy Optimization
nodes.description: PPO is the modern standard actor-critic method, preventing dangerously large policy updates using bounds for stability.

nodes.title: Actor-Critic
nodes.description: An architecture learning both the policy (the actor) and the value function (the critic) simultaneously for stable training.

nodes.title: Experience Replay
nodes.description: A technique caching transitions in a buffer memory to be randomly sampled later, breaking strong correlations in training data.

nodes.title: Target Network
nodes.description: Stabilizing neural network training by freezing the target estimates and updating them slowly over time.

nodes.title: Markov Decision Process (MDP)
nodes.description: The foundational mathematical framework for modeling fully observable decision making tasks as state transitions.

nodes.title: POMDP
nodes.description: A Partially Observable MDP, describing complicated scenarios where critical state information is hidden from the agent's sensors.

nodes.title: Dynamic Programming
nodes.description: A collection of algorithms solving complex pathing problems iteratively using perfect full environment models.

nodes.title: Epsilon-Greedy Strategy
nodes.description: The simplest exploration math: take a random exploratory action with probability ε, otherwise exploit the best known action.

nodes.title: Upper Confidence Bound (UCB)
nodes.description: Exploration based on "optimism in the face of uncertainty," statistically favoring states with large uncertainty variance.

nodes.title: Entropy Bonus
nodes.description: An additive penalty encouraging exploration by mathematically rewarding the policy for taking unpredictable random distributions.

nodes.title: Off-Policy Learning
nodes.description: Algorithms capable of learning the optimal target policy while simultaneously executing a completely different exploratory behavior policy.

nodes.title: On-Policy Learning
nodes.description: Algorithms that require evaluating and improving the exact same policy that they are currently using to make live decisions.

nodes.title: REINFORCE Algorithm
nodes.description: The classic Monte Carlo policy gradient algorithm, using the complete cumulative reward at the end of an episode to map policy weights.

nodes.title: SARSA
nodes.description: State-Action-Reward-State-Action: An on-policy TD control method where values are updated using the exact action chosen in the next state.

nodes.title: Expected Return
nodes.description: Calculated as the mathematically discounted total expected accumulated reward obtained over the remaining duration of an episode.

nodes.title: Discount Factor (γ)
nodes.description: A hyperparameter weight determining the importance of future rewards compared to immediate short-term rewards.

nodes.title: Soft Actor-Critic (SAC)
nodes.description: An advanced off-policy continuous actor-critic algorithm focused inherently on maximizing expected reward alongside maximum entropy.

nodes.title: DDPG Algorithm
nodes.description: Deep Deterministic Policy Gradients: An actor-critic algorithm designed specifically for continuous and physical action domains.

nodes.title: Recurrent Neural Networks
nodes.description: A neural architecture used to inherently retain memory, heavily utilized in POMDPs to infer unobservable environment states.

nodes.title: Belief State
nodes.description: A probabilistic distribution calculation indicating which true mathematical state the agent statistically believes it is residing in.

nodes.title: Policy Iteration
nodes.description: A structural algorithm utilizing alternating steps of evaluating a current policy entirely, then improving it entirely.

nodes.title: Value Iteration
nodes.description: Directly updating the value function iteratively to calculate the absolute optimal policy aggressively in single passes.

nodes.title: Importance Sampling
nodes.description: An advanced statistical technique used to properly re-weigh samples scaling probability variables when attempting off-policy learning.

nodes.title: Clipping Objective
nodes.description: An objective function used mainly in modern PPO to strictly limit the mathematical size of policy surrogate parameter updates.
